title: anti-intellectualism and the political carnival
description: on markets that are not capitalism, ladies applauding conquistadors, trumpists discovering Palestine, religious contradictions, and the exhaustion of thinking in public.

title: City
description: A poem of Vladimir Kotlyarov from Pornofilmy

title: Dancing to the Tune of Distraction
description: A Senate session collapses into theater, and the theater reveals what institutional politics has become.

title: Incongruity in Action
description: A march for democracy becomes a scene of class entitlement, violence, and moral theater.

title: Stańczyk and the Paradox of the Sad Clown
description: An exploration of Jan Matejko's Stańczyk and the paradox of the sad clown — finding meaning in comedy, sadness, and the inability to laugh at oneself.

title: The Cards and the Couch
description: On Tarot, Psychoanalysis, and the Things We Already Know but Cannot Say

description: On Mourning, Madness, and the Things We Cannot Put Down

title: The Gambler and the Black Square
description: A Personal Reflection

description: On Jouissance, Self-Destruction, and the Paradox of Consciousness

title: The Grand Inquisitor in the Age of Algorithms
description: Dostoevsky's Grand Inquisitor prophetically describes our algorithmic society — humanity trading freedom for bread, miracles, and authority, one dopamine hit at a time.

title: The Metamorphosis of Parsifal through "The Triumph of Death"
description: An analysis of Pieter Bruegel's masterpiece in the context of the myth of Parsifal

title: The Stranger at the Feast
description: On Being Seen, Being Misread, and the Golden Cage of Belonging

title: What Is Marxism and Why It Still Matters
description: On Class, Capital, and the Ghost That Refuses to Stay Dead

title: About
description: On the life behind this archive of literature, psychoanalysis, politics, and art.

title: Essays
description: Long-form essays on literature, psychoanalysis, politics, art, and modern life.

title: Archive Insights
description: A way of reading the archive through its rhythms, proportions, and recurring habits.

title: Motifs
description: The recurring obsessions that keep returning across the archive.

title: Listening Habits
description: The music that keeps returning while I read, walk, write, and fail to sleep.

title: Studies
description: Shorter meditations on paintings, images, and recurring visual obsessions.

title: The Bolshevik and the Weight of an Idea
description: On Revolution, Scale, and the Man Who Became Bigger Than the City

title: The Fortune Teller
description: A masterwork of Baroque realism depicting the seductive art of divination — a young Romani woman reads a nobleman's palm while subtly slipping his ring, transforming fortune telling into a metaphor for the illusionistic power of painting itself.

title: Wanderer above the Sea of Fog
description: This is a painting by Caspar David Friedrich, titled 'Wanderer above the Sea of Fog'. It depicts a man standing upon a rocky precipice with his back to the viewer. He is wrapped in a dark green overcoat, and grips a walking stick in his right hand. His hair caught in a wind, the wanderer gazes out on a landscape covered in a thick sea of fog. In the far distance, obscured by misty peaks, we can just make out what appears to be the ocean.

title: Man, Controller of the Universe and the Art of Resistance
description: On Muralism, Marxism, and the Painting That Was Destroyed and Reborn

title: Jealousy
description: Munch paints jealousy not as a love triangle, but as the humiliating position of witness.

title: Berlin Street Scene
description: On the urban gaze, commodified visibility, and the violence of modern surfaces.

title: Liberty Leading the People and the Revolutionary Tradition
description: On Revolution, Class, and the People Who Take to the Streets

title: The Soothsayer's Recompense
description: A defining work of Metaphysical Art that depicts the sleeping Ariadne in an eerie, deserted piazza — connecting divination, the unconscious, and the uncanny stillness of dreams that influenced the entire Surrealist movement.

title: Adele Bloch-Bauer I
description: On gold, assimilation, and the strange violence of being turned into ornament.

title: The Garden of Earthly Delights
description: On paradise, compulsion, and the slow slide from delight into psychic catastrophe.

title: Unexpected Visitors
description: On return, recognition, and the unbearable pause between appearance and acceptance.

title: Stańczyk
description: On the clown who sees the truth of the room and can no longer join its laughter.

title: The Treasures of Satan
description: A monumental work by the Belgian Symbolist and Theosophist Jean Delville, depicting Satan hovering over an oceanic abyss of intertwined souls — a visual representation of the unconscious depths where desires and illusions hold the psyche captive.

title: Black Suprematic Square and the Intrusive Thoughts
description: A Deep Dive into the Abyss of Despair

title: The Cyclops
description: Odilon Redon transforms the myth of Polyphemus into a luminous vision of longing and mystery — the single enormous eye becoming an archetypal symbol of inner vision, clairvoyance, and the mystical gaze into the unseen.

title: Guernica
description: An exploration of Pablo Picasso's Guernica and its relevance to the fascist dictatorship of its time.

title: The Triumph of Death
description: Bruegel's terror lies not in massacre alone, but in the abolition of any possible outside.

title: Inspection of the Old House
description: A dim interior becomes a meditation on belated return and the impossibility of inventorying loss.

title: The Demon Seated
description: Vrubel's Demon is less a monster than a figure of compressed exile and self-containment.
